title: Take a Seat in the Harvard MBA Case Classroom
body:
None of them in the class have laptops, phones or any kind of internet gadget sources. They are purely making decisions, perspectives, and opinions manually. Which is amazing!
Maybe in future I'll be seated on those benches!
author: @somone82

title: Take a Seat in the Harvard MBA Case Classroom
body: Today I phisically attended one of the MBA classes at Harvard as a visitor (you can do too, it is open). I thought the video was probably one of the most successful classes that can be shown publicly. But the real lesson was even better than this. Atmosphere, teaching, coordination, students are just unbelieveable. Around 70-80% students out of 95 (!) spoke at least once. The teacher did a great job in coordination. There was 20 minute summary presentation by her, which was cherry on top. I can't believe just a casual lesson can be in such a high quality
author: @qudratxojamusayev6785

title: Take a Seat in the Harvard MBA Case Classroom
body: She’s such a vibrant and inspiring professor. This is a class room culture you don’t see very often and this is beautiful.
author: @Highland510

title: Take a Seat in the Harvard MBA Case Classroom
body: To those whom isn’t lucky to be there, become an entrepreneur today. You will learn from real life, experiences, failures, mistakes, struggles, tears, successes, personal growth, everything. This is very motivating as I journey through my Schooling at Fort Hays State University, while I work full-time in Supply Chain. Hats off to you Josh, and all the other parents here going through this same type of adventure!

title: At 22, I was the youngest Indian to make it to Harvard! To DONATE visit link in description #donate
body:
“Mom told me, ‘We named you Muskaan because you were born with a beautiful smile!’ And true to my name, I grew up smiling. But during middle school, my smile disappeared. 
Mom’s government job demanded she’d work in different cities & I’d have to change schools frequently; I had no friends. This caused me to retreat into a shell & become a target for the bullies. At 13, I lost my sense of self; I hid my pain from mom too… 
Around the same time, mom got transferred to Chandigarh; I grew anxious about attending a new school. But thankfully, my school had a counselor for students to talk to about their mental health. 
The hardest step was going to my counselor’s office & saying – I need help. But to hear him reply, ‘I’m here for you’ felt comforting. 
And so, I started visiting my counselor & with his guidance, I took steps towards being positive. I began volunteering with various NGOs & formed meaningful friendships! And as I healed on the inside, the happier I was on the outside…
After 2 years of counseling, my grades improved too. And after I topped my college, I realized I wanted to do for other kids what my counselor did for me – I, too, wanted to become a counselor.
And just then, I came across the Master’s course for Psychology at Harvard! I said, ‘I believe in myself’ & applied. And 2 months later, I got the best email, ‘You’re accepted to study at Harvard!’; I was the youngest Indian to get in! And the happiness in mom’s eyes was indescribable. 
However, her look was also mingled with worry. She said, ‘I’m proud of you. But I can’t afford this.’.. 
The path to Harvard has been expensive— Rs. 2.5 lakhs alone went into my application & visa fees! My mom is a single mom & the breadwinner; she’s putting down her savings for me. Still, Rs. 23 lakhs more are required for me to study there… 
I’ve worked extremely hard to get where I’ve gotten, but to go further ahead, I need your help. It took me a long time to find the will to dream, & now that I’m close, I don’t want to give up on it…” 
–
Muskaan is 22 years old & requires Rs. 23 lakhs to complete her Master’s education at Harvard. Let’s help her fulfill her dream. Donate via the link in description.

Featuring: Muskaan
author: @humansofbombay2801

title: Asking BU students which major is the biggest waste of money 🗑️#students #bostonuniversity
body: I'm a journalism major with a minor in communications. I was already good at writing but my courses prep me more for the social aspect of journalism. Like interviews, getting good sources, fact checking, how to think and write critically, and how to talk to people professionally. That stuff is gonna take me farther than just my writing will, and it would've taken me alot of trial and error if I don't learn it in college.
author: @OHGEEBEE

title: Asking BU students which major is the biggest waste of money 🗑️#students #bostonuniversity
body:
I got a bachelors in criminal justice and ended up not using it. Left the police academy cause it ended up not being my thing, plus it was 72k a year (not bad)
Left for this trucking company. In 2-3 months I got my CDL at a local trucking school, ended up with a six figure trucking job. Sometimes I feel like I wasted those 4 years going to college but it is what it is
author: @Chris_Trucker

title: Asking BU students which major is the biggest waste of money 🗑️#students #bostonuniversity
body: No major is useless. You have to make connections and network BEFORE you graduate to immediately start succeeding in your chosen field. If you only get the degree but have no network, you're going to take way longer to climb the career ladder. Do internships!

author: @Live4living

title: Asking BU students which major is the biggest waste of money 🗑️#students #bostonuniversity
body:
BFA in fine arts is actually really useful if you use it. Art school gives you tons of connections and access to contemporaries (profs and peers) as well as access to different media like printing labs, photography studios, and 3D shops. Yeah, you can be successful in the arts independently, but schooling really does help with connections and development of truly good art, both conceptually and technically, that is worthy of galleries and exhibitions. Doing it on your own takes a lot of active seeking out of peers and tools for learning, development, and inspiration.

I’m honestly so sick of people saying art degrees are worthless or that the arts are worthless because they think you can’t make money, which is false … plus, culture and social progression is literally driven by the arts soooo …
author: @Skallanni

title: Asking BU students which major is the biggest waste of money 🗑️#students #bostonuniversity

No regrets.

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body: Acceptance rate doesn't mean a school is more prestigious.  This just means they get way more applicants for the vacant spots.  If a school with 1spot got 1,000 applications their acceptance rate would be 0.001%.  If that same school received 1 applicant their rate would be 100% acceptance.
author: @iguessifyousayso

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body: Clearly this person has no clue how difficult it is to get into eg UCSD or Rutgers for computer science or engineering
author: @feralhamster2429

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body: I respectfully disagree with your opinion. No way Rutgers (#40) and UMD (#46) are tier 4 IMO. Admissions rate do not equate to quality or outcomes - and public universities are required to be accessible for their in-state residents instead of exclusive.

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body: No matter what the narrator said in this video, people were going to be mad. I'm just so glad I grew up in a household where we did all the work to do well, but we were never crazy about which school I chose in the end as long as we could afford it. I think I may have made the wrong choices in the end, but I didn't feel forced into it.

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body:
You have to base it on majors and departments

Like, Rutgers, for example, has an amazing CS and engineering department, Liberty has a great game development program

What's a tier 1 is gonna be different for me as it would for others.

Some schools surprisingly get harder to get into, the higher the reputation a department gets

Or hell, the stock of a college could rise as you get into said college (happened with me doing IT game dev at NJIT from 2012-2016, program was new but it was relatively on par with something you'd see at other game PROGRAMMING CS programs, and it blended in super well for me when I went into software engineering when I graduated)

Using NJIT as an example, you could easily get in for history or theater

But hands down would it be MUCH harder getting into their biomedical engineering program. It depends on the majors, lol

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body: Just FYI, these tiers are (generally) irrelevant if you stay and work in the US. Sure, maybe if you are from a foreign country and go back there after college and tell your neighbor "I graduated from Harvard" they'll be incredibly impressed -- but if you tell them "I graduate from Caltech" or Stanford they might not even know those universities. The reality is, Stanford is much better than Harvard in some fields while the opposite is also true. I chose UCSD for my undergrad in Neuroscience over UPenn and Cornell and it wasn't even a difficult choice -- simply speaking, the research and depth of study there was a better fit and dare I say even more prestigious. No one on the west coast, by the way, will ever undermine your academic ability if you graduated from Cal or UCLA instead of Brown or Princeton. If you are admitted to any school within the first "3 tiers" you'll have similar if not identical quality of education. Yes, going to a private university does have some advantages to consider over going to a public university but these don't often justify higher costs. It is much better to go to UCI, for example, with their Reagent's Scholarship than to Yale and graduating with $100,000+ in student debt. You can always do incredibly well in a public university and then do your PhD at a private university for "free" (PhD's are often fully funded with research stipends or TA positions)

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body: UMD is not a tier 4 school, its acceptance rate is 40% and rutgers & temple both have acceptance rates 20-30% higher than UMD yet are in the same category. It should also be noted that a public 40% accpt rate is more comp than a lot of privates aritifically inflated lower accpt rate and that can be measured by the GPA, SAT score, and income outcomes out of college which are all measured by usnews. UMD is a t45 institution and a flagship state worth its salt.

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body: This is unfortunately inaccurate since your video mixes some schools which are basically top 50-60 schools into Tier 4. Suggested improvement: list range of grades, scores, acceptance rates and geography distribution (often this says how respected the school is outside its region). Also you mistake the gap between 1,2 and 3 v 4 and 5. Your gap is actually 1-2, 3-4 (you seem to place top 60 schools into Tier 4)  and then 5. But then Tier 5 seems quite large or defies description (there is a very large range from decent private or state schools to 2-year colleges). So then you are also missing several other tiers. Also your description of "to go to college to have fun and make their family proud" and "trying a lot harder than students in Tier 4 and 5" have NO EVIDENCE. I would say those statements are unusual generalizations.

title: The Truth About Boston University UNFILTERED | Pros and Cons of Attending BU | Honest BU Review 2022
body:
BU pros and cons:
Being in a city:
- Pros:
1. Smaller city than Boston and it is easier to feel like home
2. Campus is cohesive
3. A lot of things to do
4. Harvard and MIT very close by
5. Many opportunities for internship and jobs
- Cons:
1. Construction - very noisy
2. Not traditional sport bond spirit campus - more of small knit communities where everyone do their own thing
3. Weather - very cold, rainy, and snowy
4. Safety - not a close campus, so follow general safety rules

BU community:
-Pros:
1. Everyone is very friendly
2. Student population is very diverse
3. No typical BU student - you can do whatever you want
4. BU studetns are activitsts - protective of the minorities
-Cons:
1. Huge school - ~16,000 undergraduate (cannot know everyone)
2. Disparity between the faculties and higher up BU admins (community is great but BU admins are not that great)


Academic and professors:
-Pros:
1. Professors are very knowledge in their fields (some are still working in their industry so very recent knowledges)
2. Professors are very supportitve
3. BU are flexible with you taking courses outside of your major
-Cons:
1. career centered (can be a pro depends on how you look at it)
2. General education requirement (new so kind of troll right now)
3. grade deflation
4. BU faculties not as diverse

On Campus Living:
-Pros
1. Guarenteed for all 4 years
2. Wide variety of dorms to pick from
3. T train to get around the city with
4. Dinning hall is very good
5. Always a lot of events
6. Tons of green places to relax in
-Cons:
1. Tuition - very expensive
- don't give out a lot of merit scholarships

Thanks for the information Mira!

title: The Truth About Boston University UNFILTERED | Pros and Cons of Attending BU | Honest BU Review 2022
body:
Class of 1994 here. 👵
Your assessments are pretty much spot on. You might be blindsided your senior year by some cutthroat classmates, because they are lurking beneath the surface, but they have a tendency to get weeded out quickly. After graduation, your fellow alumni will drop things into your lap as long as you are willing to work hard. If you choose to stay in the Boston area, you can make a lot of money, but be aware that there is a rivalry with the other schools and Big H usually wins at the workplace.

title: if MIT were honest  #americancollege #mit #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague
body:
“weird and quirky, lol, random xD”
actual things at mit:
- clothing optional dorm floors with “exercise poles” installed
- explosives in the dorm courtyard
- annual “virgin sacrifice to the dark lord krotus” themed party
- wrestling in a pit of glitter lube or fake blood with a tesla coil blasting music
- annual piano drop, where a piano is dropped off of the roof
- real clubs include mixology, chocolate science, spinning balls of fire around your body as a performance art, and something called “assassins’ guild”
- decades old milk (the dorm pet)
- multiple dorms where students can paint literally whatever they want — from renditions of renoir to explicit materials to a fully black hallway with red lights
- before my time but a dorm floor once got in trouble for making a giant concrete replica of a bomb to promote a party called “drink ‘til you drop”
- also before my time, over half the lsd on the east coast was once made in an mit dorm
- also also before my time but us copyright law had to be rewritten because of an undergrad’s pet project pirating a ton of books and stuff

measuring bridges in smoots and putting police cars on the roof is honestly kinda tame for us tbh
author: @kimcoleeppling8177
